Items.Title: 探索 SVG 图形第五部分 | Geeking-out on SVG Graphics part-five
Items.Description:
我的项目是为软件工程师创建一个类似于切割垫样式的基于 SVG 的背景（好吧，我，不是其他任何人，为自私而道歉）。

如前一篇文章所述，我在工程师蓝色的背景颜色 (#191662) 之上绘制了一个灰色 (#777) 网格。 还有一条副线，对创作共用许可的引用，以及在最新更新中，一些常见屏幕分辨率的渲染。

在这篇倒数第二篇文章中，我将添加一些与以下美学比率对齐的额外径向线。

审美比例比率公式
黄金比例（又名斐波那契） 白银比率毕达哥拉斯斜边平方（2）
维特鲁威系数黄金和白银比率对艺术和建筑的影响有据可查，但其他比率可能不太熟悉。

给定一个长度为 1 的单位直角三角形，最长边（称为斜边）的长度为 1.414，由公式 sqrt(2) 计算得出。

我将在每个阶段结束时通过我的GitHub存储库发布切割垫 SVG 图像。

审美比率

所有四个比率都将绘制为从左下角辐射的线（坐标 x:0，y:800）。 每个比率的终点将通过将网格的高度（即 800px）乘以该比率并从原点减去它来计算，如下所示。

审美比例比率宽度
黄金比例 (GR) 白银比率 (SR) 毕达哥拉斯斜边 (PH) 维特鲁威系数 (VC) 最大宽度为 1280 像素，因此只有 VC 和 PH 会与网格顶部相交（Y = 0）。 比率 GR 和 SR 必须与右边缘相交，因此需要 Y 坐标的替代值。

与上一篇文章中的标准角度一样，有弧线将线与边缘连接起来，但代替所示角度的正弦值，我展示了每个美学比率的值。

维特鲁威系数 (VC)

正如本文开头所承诺的，我现在将解释我称之为“维特鲁威系数”的比率背后的想法。 它基于列奥纳多·达·芬奇 (Leonardo da Vinci) 的名为《维特鲁威人》(The Vitruvian Man) 的图表，其中有两个男人的代表，一个覆盖另一个，手臂和腿伸展开来。 两个图形都包含在一个正方形和一个圆圈内，每个图形都接触一个或另一个。

当我小时候第一次看到这个图表时，它向我提出了一个问题，我只是在最近才计算出一个答案。

我的问题是，给定相同区域的正方形和圆形，它们之间是否存在关系：

  • 外部（周长v周长）
  • 宽度（图 v 侧） 但答案比我预期的更令人惊讶。

如果两个形状的面积 (A) 均为 120m，则正方形的尺寸为：

  • 边 (S) = SQRT(A) = 10.95
  • 周长 (P) = 4 x S = 43.82

圆的尺寸是：

  • 面积 = PI * R * R，因此，SQRT(A / PI) = R
  • 直径 (D) = 2 * SQRT(A / PI) = 12.36
  • 周长 (C) = D * PI = 38.83

但是当我继续比较外部和宽度时，真正让我感到惊讶的是。

  • 周长 / 周长 = 43.82 / 38.83 = 1.128（向下取整）
  • 直径/边 = 12.36 / 10.95 = 1.128（向下取整）

两个方程的结果相同，但这里有一个技巧。 对于外部计算，我们使用了正方形在圆上的尺寸。 对于宽度，我们使用正方形上的圆的尺寸。 不要问我为什么，我不是数学家，但是当我手工计算方程时，发现近似值 1.128 被确认为 2/SQRT(PI)。

那么VC有什么用呢？

对于面积相同的正方形和圆形，
好吧，如果你有一个正方形并且你想要一个面积相同的圆，你可以将 VC 乘以正方形的边来得到圆的直径。 如果你有一个圆并且你想要一个正方形，你可以将圆的周长乘以 VC 得到正方形的周长。

我知道这不是惊天动地的，但我发现它很有趣并且可能很有用。



原文:

My project is to create an SVG-based backdrop in the style of a cutting mat for software engineers (well me, not really anyone else, apologies for being selfish).

As covered in the previous posts, I have a grid drawn in grey (#777) on top of a background colour (#191662) of Engineer's blue. There is also a by-line, a reference to creative-commons licencing and, in the latest update, a rendering of some common screen resolutions.

In this penultimate post I will be adding some additional radial lines aligned to the following Aesthetic Ratios.

Aesthetic ratio Ratio Formulae
Golden Ratio (aka Fibonacci) 1.618 (1 + sqrt(5))/2
Silver Ratio 2.414 (1 + sqrt(2))
Pythagorean Hypotenuse 1.414 sqrt(2)
Vitruvian Coefficient 1.128 2/sqrt(pi)

The effect of the Golden and Silver ratios on art and architecture are well documented but the other ratios might not be quite so familiar.

Given a unit right-angle triangle with two sides of length 1, the longest side (known as the hypotenuse) will have a length of 1.414 as calculated by the formula sqrt(2).

The "Vitruvian Coefficient" is a ratio all of my own creation, which I will explain, for those that might be interested, later in this post.

I will release the cutting-mat SVG image at the end of each stage via my GitHub repo.

Aesthetic Ratios

All four ratios will be plotted as a line radiating from the bottom-left corner (coordinate x:0, y:800). The end point of each ratio will be calculated by multiplying the height of the grid (which is 800px) by the ratio and deducting it from the origin as follows.

Aesthetic ratio Ratio Width
Golden Ratio (GR) 1.618 1294
Silver Ratio (SR) 2.414 1931
Pythagorean Hypotenuse (PH) 1.414 1131
Vitruvian Coefficient (VC) 1.128 902

The maximum width is 1280px so only VC and PH will intersect with the top of the grid (Y = 0). The ratios GR and SR will have to intersect with the right edge so alternative values for the Y coordinate will be required.

GR(Y) = 1280 / 1.618 = 791, deducted from the origin = 9px.
SR(Y) = 1280 / 2.414 = 530, offset = 270px.

As with the Standard angles in the previous post, there are arcs connecting the line with the edge but in place of the Sine of the angle being shown I have presented the value of each aesthetic ratio.

Vitruvian Coefficient (VC)

As promised at the beginning of this post, I will now explain my thinking behind the ratio I call the "Vitruvian Coefficient". It is based on Leonardo da Vinci's diagram called The Vitruvian Man where there are two representations of a man, one overlaying the other with arms and legs stretched out. Both figured are enclosed within a square and a circle with each figure touching one or the other.

When I first saw the diagram as a child it raised a question in me that for which I have only relatively recently calculated an answer.

My query is, given a square and a circle of the same area, is there a relationship between their:

  • outsides (circumference v perimeter)
  • width (diagram v side) But the answer was even more surprising than I was expecting.

If the area (A) of both shapes is 120m the dimensions of the square are:

  • Side (S) = SQRT(A) = 10.95
  • Perimeter (P) = 4 x S = 43.82

The dimensions of the circle are:

  • Area = PI * R * R, therefore, SQRT(A / PI) = R
  • Diameter (D) = 2 * SQRT(A / PI) = 12.36
  • Circumference (C) = D * PI = 38.83

But the real surprise for me came when I went on to compare the outsides and the widths.

  • Perimeter / Circumference = 43.82 / 38.83 = 1.128 (rounded down)
  • Diameter / Side = 12.36 / 10.95 = 1.128 (rounded down)

Both equations have the same result but there was a trick here. For the outside calculation we used the dimension of the square over the circle. For the width we used the dimension of the circle over the square. Don't ask me why, I am not a mathematician, but when I worked the equations out by hand to find the approximate value 1.128 is confirmed to be 2/SQRT(PI).

So what use is VC?

For a square and circle of the same area,
VC = Perimeter / Circumference = Diameter / Side = 2/SQRT(PI)

Well, if you have a square and you want a circle of the same area you can multiply VC by the Side of the square to get the Diameter of the circle. If you have a circle and you want a square you can multiply the Circumference of the circle by VC to get the Perimeter of the square.

Not Earth-shattering I know but I found it interesting and it could be useful.
Items.Author: tracygjg

Items.Title: 通过使用 defer 以最佳方式加载 JavaScript。 | Load JavaScript optimally by utilizing defer.
Items.Description:
在现代网站上，脚本比 HTML heavier ：它们需要更长的下载和处理时间。 每当浏览器加载 HTML 并遇到script标签时，它就无法继续生成 DOM（文档对象模型）。 外部脚本的处理方式也类似：浏览器必须先等待脚本下载，然后运行下载的脚本，然后再继续处理其余部分。

这是迄今为止最重要和最棒的功能，也是您应该如何加载 JavaScript 的方式，所以让我们从我们非常小的演示开始吧。

因此，首先，让我们从创建两个文件开始，一个名为index.html ，另一个名为script.js ，在index.html文件中，使用HTML snippets扩展添加一些简单的样板代码，或者您可以简单地复制代码下文提及。

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang= "en" > <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" > <meta http-equiv= "X-UA-Compatible" content= "IE=edge" > <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" > <title> Script: defer,async </title> </head> <body> </body> </html>

现在，在我们的身体内部，我们将制作一个带有一些文本的简单按钮，然后在我们的头脑中，我们将添加一个脚本标签并导入我们刚刚准备好的script.js文件。

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang= "en" > <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" > <meta http-equiv= "X-UA-Compatible" content= "IE=edge" > <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" > <title> Script: defer,async </title> <script src= "./scripts.js" ></script> </head> <body> <button> Hello There! </button> </body> </html>

完成此操作后，使用实时服务器在浏览器中打开该索引文件，您会注意到我们只有一个非常简单的按钮。

让我们添加一些简单的脚本来操作我们的按钮，因此将以下 JavaScript 代码复制并粘贴到您的script.js文件中。

 // scripts.js const body = document . querySelector ( ' body ' ); const btn = document . querySelector ( ' button ' ); btn . style . display = ' block ' ; btn . style . position = ' absolute ' ; btn . style . left = ' 50% ' ; btn . style . top = ' 50% ' ; btn . style . transform = ' translate(-50%, -50%) ' ; btn . style . width = ' 200px ' ; btn . style . height = ' 100px ' ; btn . style . fontSize = ' 2rem ' ; btn . style . fontFamily = ' sans-serif ' ; btn . style . borderRadius = ' 10% ' ; btn . style . backgroundColor = ' #8661d1 ' ; btn . style . color = ' #fff ' ; btn . style . border = ' none ' ; btn . style . outline = ' none ' ; btn . style . cursor = ' pointer ' ; btn . style . boxShadow = ' 0 0 10px #000 ' ; btn . style . transition = ' all 0.5s ease-in-out ' ; btn . addEventListener ( ' mouseenter ' , () => { btn . style . backgroundColor = ' #FD5252 ' ; }); btn . addEventListener ( ' mouseleave ' , () => { btn . style . backgroundColor = ' #8661d1 ' ; }); btn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , () => { body . style . backgroundColor = ' #FD5252 ' ; setTimeout (() => { body . style . backgroundColor = ' #fff ' ; }, 1000 ); }); btn . addEventListener ( ' transitionend ' , () => { btn . style . backgroundColor = ' #8661d1 ' ; });

因此，在这个脚本中，我们首先选择了body ，然后是button ，然后我们为该按钮应用了一系列样式，然后我们继续添加一些事件侦听器，最后我们说明了该按钮在特定事件中应该做什么，就是这样，我们的按钮已经完全使用 JavaScript 的绝对力量进行控制。

如果你刷新你的浏览器，你会注意到没有任何变化，因为我们的脚本在头部，这通常是不好的，因为这意味着你的 JavaScript 是在正文本身之前加载的。 要解决这个问题，只需在script标签内添加一个defer即可。 我们在这里所做的是，我们只是延迟了我们的脚本，这意味着我们的脚本确保我们的整个身体在运行我们的任何 JavaScript 之前加载。 因此，当您完成此操作后，您的index.html文件应如下所示。

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang= "en" > <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" > <meta http-equiv= "X-UA-Compatible" content= "IE=edge" > <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" > <title> Script: defer,async </title> <script src= "./scripts.js" defer ></script> </head> <body> <button> Hello There! </button> </body> </html>

现在转到您的浏览器并重新加载它，您应该会看到与此类似的内容。

如果我们去掉这个defer ，你会发现我们的按钮不起作用； 相反，我们得到一个错误，说cannot read property style of null 。

我们会收到这个错误，因为当我们到达脚本标签时，它会停止解析我们的 html 的其余部分，但会继续执行脚本，并且只有在此之后它才会继续解析，这意味着该按钮从未添加到我们的页面中。 但是，如果我们将defer放在 script 标签中，它会下载所有 JavaScript，然后继续渲染所有内容，因此它首先渲染我们的按钮，然后在下载脚本后开始渲染整个页面。

人们经常将脚本放在底部，这很好用，但这样做的问题是，直到浏览器呈现整个页面后才会开始下载脚本，这意味着它可能会减慢您网站的加载速度，并且可能使网站杂乱无章，没有吸引力。

因此，只需将 JavaScript 放在头部并确保设置了defer ，您就不必等待 DOM 事件加载，也不必处理将 JavaScript 放在正文末尾的不便性质，这是乏味和可怕的。

所以，从现在开始，把你的剧本head 。 您所要做的就是defer它，它的作用与将脚本放在底部一样，但它会帮助您的网页或网站加载得更快。



原文:

Scripts are heavier than HTML on modern sites: they take longer to download and process. Whenever the browser loads HTML and encounters a script tag, it is unable to continue generating the DOM(Document Object Model). External scripts are also treated similarly: the browser must first wait for the script to download, then run the downloaded script before continuing to process the rest.

This is by far the most significant and fantastic feature, and it's how you should load your JavaScript, so let's get started with our very tiny little demonstration.

So, initially, let's start by creating two files, one called index.html and the other called script.js, and inside the index.html file, add some simple boilerplate code by using the HTML snippets extension or you can simply copy the code mnetioned below.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Script: defer,async</title>
</head>
<body>
</body>
</html>

Now, inside our body, we'll just make a simple button with some text on it, and then inside our head, we'll add a script tag and import our script.js file that we just prepared.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Script: defer,async</title> <script src="./scripts.js"></script>
</head> <body> <button>Hello There!</button>
</body> </html>

Once you've done that, open that index file in your browser with the live server, and you'll notice that we only have a very simple button.

Let's add some simple script to manipulate our button, so copy and paste the following JavaScript code into your script.js file.

// scripts.js
const body = document.querySelector('body');
const btn = document.querySelector('button'); btn.style.display = 'block';
btn.style.position = 'absolute';
btn.style.left = '50%';
btn.style.top = '50%';
btn.style.transform = 'translate(-50%, -50%)';
btn.style.width = '200px';
btn.style.height = '100px';
btn.style.fontSize = '2rem';
btn.style.fontFamily = 'sans-serif';
btn.style.borderRadius = '10%';
btn.style.backgroundColor = '#8661d1';
btn.style.color = '#fff';
btn.style.border = 'none';
btn.style.outline = 'none';
btn.style.cursor = 'pointer';
btn.style.boxShadow = '0 0 10px #000';
btn.style.transition = 'all 0.5s ease-in-out';
btn.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { btn.style.backgroundColor = '#FD5252';
});
btn.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { btn.style.backgroundColor = '#8661d1';
});
btn.addEventListener('click', () => { body.style.backgroundColor = '#FD5252'; setTimeout(() => { body.style.backgroundColor = '#fff'; }, 1000);
});
btn.addEventListener('transitionend', () => { btn.style.backgroundColor = '#8661d1';
});

So, in this script, we first selected the body, then the button, then we apply a range of styles to that button, and then we proceed to add some event listeners, and finally we stated what that button should do in a specific event, and that's it, our button has been totally controlled using the absoluet power of JavaScript.

If you refresh your browser, you will notice no any changes that is because our script is up in the head, which is normally bad because it means that your JavaScript is loading way before the body itself. To fix this, simply add a defer inside the script tag. What we did here is, we simply deferred our script, which means that our script is ensuring that our entire body loads before running any of our JavaScript. So, when you've done that, your index.html file should look like this.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Script: defer,async</title> <script src="./scripts.js" defer></script>
</head> <body> <button>Hello There!</button>
</body> </html>

Now go to your browser and reload it, and you should see something similar to this.

If we remove this defer, you'll find that our button doesn't work; instead, we get an error that says cannot read property style of null.

We'll get this error because when we get to our script tag, it stops parsing the rest of our html, but proceeds to the script, and only after that it continues parsing, which means the button never got added to our page. However, if we put defer in the script tag, it downloads all the JavaScript but then continues rendering everything, so it renders our button first, then begins rendering the complete page once the script has been downloaded.

People frequently put the script at the bottom, which works just fine, but the problem with this is that the download for the script doesn't start until the browser renders the entire page, which means it could slow down your website's loading speed and might make the site disorganized and unappealing.

Hence, by simply placing your JavaScript in the head and ensuring that defer is set, you will never have to wait for DOM events to load, nor will you have to deal with the inconvenient nature of placing your JavaScript towards the end of your body, which is tedious and awful.

So, from now on, keep your script at the head. All you have to do is just defer it, and it will do the same thing as putting your script towards the bottom, but it will help your webpage or website load even more faster.

Items.Title: 松弛的 OAuth | OAuth with slack
Items.Description:
尼基尔·德瓦拉塞蒂

发表于使用 slack，我们可以为所需的范围实现 OAuth，以访问工作区通道、成员、对话、呼叫、监听事件等......

哦，🤔 那么范围是什么？ 范围用于授予您的应用程序在 Slack 中执行功能的权限，例如，

  1. 调用 Web API 方法、users.list、channels.read、channels.write 等...
  2. 接收事件 API 事件，如用户加入、频道创建等...

那么该怎么做呢？ 创建应用程序并将其安装到工作区：

  1. 通过转到https://slack.com创建 slack 工作区
  2. 之后，通过转到https://api.slack.com/apps并选择相应的工作区来创建一个应用程序。
  3. 创建应用程序后，转到创建的应用程序并在OAuth & permissions部分下提供所需的范围。
  4. 提供范围后，您可以将应用程序安装到您的工作区或将其发布到松弛的开放目录。

执行 OAuth

  1. 要执行 OAuth，我们需要生成授权重定向 url，为此导航到https://api.slack.com/authentication/sign-in-with-slack

  2. 网址看起来像，

     GET /openid/connect/authorize? response_type=code &scope=openid%20profile%20email &client_id=s6BhdRkqt3 &state=af0ifjsldkj &team=T1234 &nonce=abcd &redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fclient.example.org%2Fcb HTTP/1.1 Host: https://slack.com

  3. 上面 url 中的redirect_uri是 slack 使用临时访问代码重定向的请求处理程序。

  4. 使用该临时访问代码，我们需要使用 api slack.com/api/openid.connect.token?code={access_code}&client_id=###&client_secret=###&redirect_uri=###请求访问令牌，以获取更多信息有关 api 方法、标头和参数的详细信息，请查看收到访问令牌后，您可以安全地存储它并在需要时使用它。

欢呼！ 完成了😇

结论：

  • 我提供了基本的演练，请浏览https://api.slack.com/authentication/basics以获取详细说明。
  • 并探索刷新令牌概念，如何选择它，使用刷新令牌刷新访问令牌。

迪娜·安德兰德拉娜 - May 21 5月21日



原文:

Nikhil Devarasetty

Posted on May 22

🤔 What is OAuth? 👇
OAuth is a protocol that lets your app request authorization to private details.

With slack we can implement OAuth for the required scopes, to access the workspace channels, members, conversations, calls, listen to events, etc...

Ohh, 🤔 What are scopes then? 👇
Scopes are used to grant your app permission to perform functionality in Slack like,

  1. Call Web API methods, users.list, channels.read, channels.write, etc...
  2. Receive Events API events like, user joined, channel created, etc...

Then how to do it? 👉 Follow the below steps

Create and Install app to workspace:

  1. Create slack workspace, by going to https://slack.com
  2. After that, create an app, by going to https://api.slack.com/apps, choosing respective workspace.
  3. After creating app, go to the created app and under OAuth & permissions section give the required scopes.
  4. After giving scopes you can able to install app to your workspace or publish it to slack open directory.

Performing OAuth

  1. To perform OAuth we need to generate authorization redirect url, to do so navigate to https://api.slack.com/authentication/sign-in-with-slack

  2. The url will look like,

    GET /openid/connect/authorize?
 response_type=code
 &scope=openid%20profile%20email
 &client_id=s6BhdRkqt3
 &state=af0ifjsldkj
 &team=T1234
 &nonce=abcd
 &redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fclient.example.org%2Fcb 
HTTP/1.1
Host: https://slack.com

  3. The redirect_uri in above url, is the request handler for which slack to redirect with temporary access code.

  4. With that temporary access code we need to request for access-token using api slack.com/api/openid.connect.token?code={access_code}&client_id=###&client_secret=###&redirect_uri=###, for more details about api method, headers and parameter look into https://api.slack.com/methods/openid.connect.token

  5. With the access-token received, you can store it securely and use it whenever required.

Hurray! It's done 😇

Conclusion:

  • I have provided basic walkthrough, please do explore https://api.slack.com/authentication/basics, for detailed explanation.
  • And explore the Refresh token concept, how to opt for it, refresh the access-token using refresh token.

Dina Andrandraina - May 21

Items.Title: 学习 Golang - 第 1 天 | Learning Golang - day 1
Items.Description:
我刚决定重新开始学习围棋。 去年秋天我学了一点，但后来我的个人生活过得很艰难，还决定开始寻找一份使用 vanilla JavaScript 的前端工作。 我找到了一份工作，我可以在工作中学到任何我需要的东西。 所以在我的空闲时间，下班后，我想专注于一些不同的事情。

我的计划是每天学习一点，最好做一些笔记。 我有很多资料——书籍和在线课程。 我还在文档中阅读了很多关于 Go 文档和教程的内容。 为了不与分析瘫痪作斗争，我想从 Head First Go 开始一次使用一个资源。

我今天学到的东西：

这意味着当我们创建一个变量时，我们应该声明它的类型（string、boolean、int、float64 等），虽然我们可以更改变量的值，但我们不能稍后在程序中更改它的类型。

这样，每个变量的值为 0（对于字符串 0 表示空字符串，对于布尔值则为 false）

现在，它知道 city 是一个字符串，length 是一个 float64

如果我们想要进行数学计算，我们不能在 Go 中将整数添加到浮点数。 我们需要将整数转换为浮点数。 我们也可以将浮点数转换为整数，但它可以更改其值。

 allApples := 3 eatenApples := 0.5 applesLeft := float64(allApples) - eatenApples fmt.Println(applesLeft)

否则，我们会出错。 相同的导入但未使用的包。

但是，如果我们想让我们的包在另一个程序中使用成为可能，并使一些变量在那里可用，我们需要从大写开始。

到目前为止，我一直在使用“fmt”包来打印我的代码输出，这要归功于 Println 方法。

这主要是我在秋天学到的东西的重复。 但我认为这是一个好的开始。



原文:

I just decided to start learning Go again. I was learning it a bit last autumn, but then I had a very rough time in my personal life and also decided to start looking for a frontend job with vanilla JavaScript. I got a job where I can learn on the job anything I need for it. So in my free time, after work, I want to focus on something a bit different.

My plan is to learn a bit each day and preferably take some notes. I have quite a lot of materials - books, and online courses. I also read a lot good about Go documentation and tutorial in the docs. In order not to struggle with analysis paralysis, I want to use one resource at a time starting from Head First Go.

Things I learned today:

1. Main structure of a file in Go: 

package main
// the name of the package - we call the function the same import ( "fmt" "reflect"
) // Here we import other packages we are going to use in our code. func main() { fmt.Println("Hello, Go!") fmt.Println(reflect.TypeOf("Hello, Go!"))
} // Then goes the function with its body between moustache brackets.

2. Go is a strictly typed language.

It means that when we are creating a variable we should declare its type (string, boolean, int, float64 and so on) and while we can change the value of the variable, we can't change its type later on in the program.

3. There are at least 3 ways of declaring variables:

- we can declare a variable without assigning any value to it

var name string;
var age int;
var price float64;

This way, the value of each variable is 0 (for strings 0 means an empty string, for boolean it's false)

** - when we declare a variable and assign a value, we don't have to declare the type because Go recognizes the type from the value**

var city = "Warsaw"
var length = 3.5

Now, it knows that city is a string and length is a float64

** - while declaring the variable with its value, we can use a shorter declaration form**

quantity := 4
fruit := "apple"

4. We can convert types.

If we want to make mathematical calculations we can't add an integer to a float number in Go. We need to convert an integer into a float number. We can also convert a float number into an integer but it can change its value.

allApples := 3
eatenApples := 0.5
applesLeft := float64(allApples) - eatenApples
fmt.Println(applesLeft)

5. If we declare a variable, we need to use it.

Otherwise, we will have an error. The same imported but unused packages.

6. Variables and functions usually start with a lowercase.

But if we want to make it possible to use our package in another program and make some of the variables available there, we need to start them from an uppercase.

So far I've been using "fmt" package for printing the output of my code and it was possible thanks to the Println method.

It's been mostly the repetition of what I learned in the autumn. But I think it's a good start.
Items.Author: makneta

Items.Title: 我是如何摆脱训练营后的低迷状态的 | How I got myself out of the post-Bootcamp Slump
Items.Description:
训练营结束后，我感到非常有动力找到第一份开发人员工作。 我已经接受了采访，我觉得这只是时间问题。 经历了多轮面试，到了最后的面试阶段（四次面试之后），才发现我被一个更有经验的人淘汰了。 说我的自尊心受到打击是轻描淡写的。 它可能更接近四车碰撞。

所以我尝试充电并再试一次。 我认为我必须重新学习编码训练营教给我的所有内容。 一遍又一遍地阅读教学代码，直到我认为我可以以某种方式无所不知。 如果我只是疯狂地学习，我可以轻松地炫耀我的技能。 但事实并非如此。

夏令营结束后你会得到很多杂项建议。 专注于算法。 专注于技术面试。 不，专注于 70% 的项目/算法，30% 的人在寻找工作。 这么多混杂的信息，我真的觉得有点失落，我自己一个人。 有趣的是，我以为我已经把它放在包里了，但是在训练营之后我至少花了一个月的时间才真正为求职制定了一个可靠的计划。

第一个月，我审查了所有内容。 我很熟练，但没有一个大师。 面试即将到来，但我没有取得任何进展。 我的技术面试还有很多不足之处，当我终于开始练习我的算法时，面试就停止了。 申请和等待面试的情绪损失很大。 随着时间的流逝，我越是感到无助，冒名顶替综合症就越是蔓延。

最后，经过多次同情的聚会，我决定采取一种不同的、更友善的方式。 这是我在训练营后学到的，帮助我摆脱了低迷。

我的编码训练营是在线的，我已经习惯了在小组环境中的能量，并依靠其他人的能量来帮助我完成紧张的课程作业。 我们有四个多月的时间（每周 70-90 小时）。 这真的很激烈，但我们都非常专注并准备互相帮助。 新兵训练营结束后，精力旺盛的梦想破灭了，我又回到了开始的地方。 独自一人，不确定下一步该做什么，希望课程或教室的结构能指导我完成下一个求职阶段。 这当然没有发生，我发现自己坐在电脑椅上盯着算法可能有半个小时，然后就分心了。 找点东西吃，和小狗玩耍，或者只是在抖音上滚动以分散注意力。 一个月后，这是我自营以来最没效率的一个月，我看到有些事情必须改变。

一个暗示告诉我，它是从我的环境开始的。 我一个人在家太放松太舒服了，没有人可以追究我的责任。 所以我早上去星巴克，晚上去我当地的图书馆。 我开始为自己制定一个帮助我集中注意力的例行程序，即使我没有这样做的动力。 刚走出舒适的房子，我就开始需要有目标了。

动机是一个有趣的东西。 你认为它来自某个地方。 它深埋在我们身体的某个地方，我们只需要等待它再次回来。 或者我们一开始会感到大量的动力，当我们失去它时，我们只需要找回它来真正让事情重新开始。 不，不要依靠动力来帮助您完成下一阶段的求职之旅。

我曾经听有人说过，动机只占成功的 10%。 其他 90% 来自纪律。 出于某种原因，这让我很震惊。 我的动力空前低落，但我对纪律的需求空前高涨。 我知道我需要改变一些东西。 仅凭这一例行公事就无法将我带到我需要去的地方。 某种类型的目标，无论大小，都是让我进入下一阶段所必需的。 所以我对自己进行了合理化。 如果我没有动力，我意识到在训练营后很早就开始了，那么我需要从其他地方获取能量。 纪律对我来说就像珠穆朗玛峰，所以我必须知道我是谁以及我能处理什么来接近它。

每天设定三个目标。 这条经验法则让我度过了许多艰难时期。 您只需完成您知道可以完成的三件事的想法为您的成功奠定了基础。 三目标规则是一条黄金法则，因为它可以平衡许多人挣扎的两个极端。 第一个极端是极端动机。 在我感到有动力的日子里，我想征服代码世界。 确保我一天中的所有时间都在工作，只休息吃饭，也许看一个 30 分钟的节目（吃饭时），然后工作到晚上。 作为一个正在恢复的完美主义者，我的目标是设定非常高的目标，或者有我认为不是很高但实际上非常高的期望。

第二个极端则完全相反，极度压抑的精力和低落的动力。 我什么都不想做，什么都不想，只看我最喜欢的节目的重播。 我设定的任何目标都可能只会帮助我拖延更多，并且因为这样做而感觉更糟。 这就是三个进球的美妙之处。 如果您正确设置它们，它可以对抗这两种态度。

你设定的三个目标必须是你知道你可以完成的事情，即使你有动力去做更多，和/或根本没有动力去做任何事情。 一开始它设置的门槛很低，让你的身心适应新的结构。 这就像健美。 开始时，您不会从 50 磅或 3 磅的重量开始，而是从您知道可以捡起三组的东西开始。 随着时间的推移，你可以慢慢增加阻力，这样就不会伤到自己。 完成三个目标后，您将在一天中剩下的时间里得到奖励，而不会感到任何内疚。 它确实是一个救生员，所以明智地选择你的目标，然后开始。

在给我的无数建议中，有一个对我来说最突出。 专注于一个大项目，掌握它，并向潜在雇主展示它。 它可能不适合所有人，但这对我来说是最好的建议，因为它要求做我喜欢的一件事，那就是编码。 所以，我才开始编码。 在我的脑海中并没有太多可靠的应用程序创意，但我选择了一些我知道我会喜欢用我很高兴学习更多的编程语言工作的东西。

它也减轻了我做算法的压力。 它迫使我有一个大目标，我知道随着时间的推移我可以一次完成一小步。 当然这并不容易，我的工作已经为我完成了。 我对我的应用程序的愿景非常宏大，但我很有动力，即使我没有动力，我也期待着开发新代码。 我对解决技术问题的热爱帮助我为我的网站创建了很棒的新功能，所有这些都是我自己学到的。

另一个好处是，它增加了我在面试时与经理交谈时的信心。 我从一开始就在信心中挣扎，但在面试结束并带着一些东西回来后，我更有信心了。 我做了一件我认为自己缺乏的事情，那就是能力。 我提高了我在这门语言中的能力，并且知道我可以说得很好。 它还给了我学习新的和困难的代码的经验，这是我在训练营没有学到的。 我是如何在调试时解决问题的，这种情况经常发生，并克服了我的代码中的障碍。 我为我的网站感到自豪，并为我能够做到的事情感到自豪。

我曾经听说女性编码员最难解决这个问题。 他们往往倾向于低估自己的代码能力，并觉得自己不如男性同行知识渊博。 我不确定这是否属实，但我知道我经常低估自己作为编码员的能力，有时觉得自己受教育程度不够。 尽管我花了无数时间学习新代码并创建了出色的应用程序，但冒名顶替综合症是真实存在的。 当您认为自己有一个关键缺陷（没有经验）是您没有得到这份工作的原因时，这也可能令人沮丧。

这是一场艰苦的战斗，当然，我知道。 我们都在与它斗争。 尽管有时我觉得自己像个冒名顶替者，但我内心深处知道我是合格的，即使我的想法试图说服我不这样做。 我们必须在如此短的时间内完成大量学习，这是一项了不起的壮举，也证明了我们的克服能力。 仅仅四个（现在 6 个月）前，我们还不知道对象是什么，我们绝对不知道算法是什么。 我记得在训练营之前试图解释什么是算法。 男孩，我错了吗。

最后，我们都为经历了如此紧张的课程而感到惊讶，毕业是我们都应该感到自豪的事情。

如果你觉得你甚至不能接近代码，那没关系。 如果你觉得你不能接受任何人的任何建议，那也是可以理解的。 当我在调试我的网站或为我的笔记本电脑哭泣时，我一遍又一遍地重复着最好的咒语之一是这样的，

无论花多少时间都是值得的。

这是有史以来最好的口头禅，尤其是对于编码人员。 有很多次我在我的代码中连续五个多小时试图设置一项功能，却发现我错过了一个变量。 或者发现我想要并工作了三天的功能太先进了，我不得不放弃它。 我经常流泪，但我一直在重复这个口头禅，我知道最终这一切都是值得的。 即使你看不到地平线上的下一步，你也必须相信它就在那里。

你认为你在代码上浪费了三个小时或三天的时间，这是值得的。 那个时候你调试只是发现修复破坏了另一段代码，这是值得的时间。 您必须从头开始的代码是值得花时间的。 所有的。 那是因为你学到了一些东西。 即使你知道你没学过，你还是学到了一些东西。 通过这一切，你获得了编码员的经验，无论结果如何，这都是值得的。

如果没有阻力，你就无法增加肌肉，如果不犯错误和失败，你就无法成为更好的程序员。 失败是成为优秀程序员的最佳方式。

我希望这可以帮助像我一样的人。 学习这些课程就是生活的全部内容。 从失败到成功，因为这就是你成功的方式。



原文:

When the bootcamp ended, I felt super motivated to find that first developer job. I already had an interview and I felt like it was only a matter of time. Went through multiple rounds of interviews, got to the final interview stage (four interviews later), only to find that I was passed over for someone with more experience. To say my pride took a hit is an understatement. It's probably closer to a four-car collision.

So I tried to recharge and try again. I thought I had to relearn everything my coding bootcamp taught me. Going over instructional code over and over again until I thought I could somehow be all-knowing if I did. That if I just studied like crazy I could show off my skills easily. But that was not the case.

You get a lot of miscellaneous advice after camp. Focus on algos. Focus on the technical interview. No, focus on 70% projects/algos, 30% looking for jobs. So many mixed messages, I really felt kinda lost and on my own. It's funny how I thought I had it in the bag, but it took me at least a month after the bootcamp to really have a solid plan for the job hunt.

For the first month, I reviewed everything. I was skilled, but a master of none. Interviews were coming, but I wasn't making any headway. My technical interviews left much to be desired and when I finally starting practicing my algos, the interviews stopped coming. The emotional toll applying and waiting for interviews was significant. As more time passed, the more helpless I felt and the more the imposter syndrome crept in.

Finally, after many pitying parties, I decided to take a different, kinder approach. Here is what I learned after bootcamp that helped me out of my slump.

1) Get out of the house.

My coding bootcamp was online and I got used to the energy of being in a group setting and relying on others energy to get me through the intense coursework. We had (70-90hrs per week) for over four months. It was really intense, but we were all super focused and ready to help each other. After the boot camp ended, the fever dream of energy was lost and I was back where I started. Alone and unsure of what to do next, hoping the structure of a course or classroom would guide me through this next phase of the job hunt. That didn't happen of course, and I found myself in my computer chair staring at algos for maybe a half-hour, but then getting distracted. Finding something to eat, playing with the puppy, or just scrolling on tiktok for distraction. After a month of this, the most unproductive I have had ever been since camp, I saw that something had to change.

An inkling told me that it started from my environment. I was too relaxed and comfortable at home, by myself, where no one could hold me accountable. So I went to starbucks in the morning, and my local library in the evening. I started to create a routine for myself that helped me focus, even if I wasn't motivated to do so. Just getting out of the house of comfort jumpstarted my need to have goals.

2) Do NOT rely on motivation.

Motivation is a funny thing. You think it comes from somewhere. It's buried deep somewhere in our bodies and we just have to wait it out for it to come back again. Or we feel tons of motivation at first, and when we lose it, we just need to get it back to really get things moving again. No. Do not rely on motivation to get you through the next phase of the job hunt journey.

I heard someone say once that motivation only accounts for 10% of success. The other 90% comes from discipline. For some reason, that struck me. My motivation was at an all time low, but my need for discipline was at an all-time high. I knew I needed to change something. That routine alone could not get me where I needed to go. That some type of goal, be it small or large, was necessary to get me through to the next phase. So I rationalized with myself. If I wasn't going to be motivated, which I realized came fairly early after camp, then I needed to get the energy from somewhere else. Discipline seemed like Mount Everest for me so I had to approach it knowing who I was and what I could handle.

3) Set teeny tiny goals.

Set three goals a day. That rule of thumb has gotten me through many hard times. The idea that you only have to accomplish three things that you KNOW you can accomplish sets you up for success. The three goal rule is a golden rule because it can balance the two extremes that many struggle with. The first extreme is extreme motivation. For the days I felt motivated, I wanted to conquer the world of code. Making sure I worked all hours of the day, only taking breaks to eat, maybe watch a 30 min show (while eating), and then work until night. As a recovering perfectionist, my go to is setting extremely high goals, or have expectations that I think aren't very high, but are in fact very high.

The second extreme is the exact opposite, extreme depressed energy and low motivation. I want to do nothing, feel nothing, and watch reruns of my favorite shows. Any goal I set would probably only serve to help me procastinate more and feel worse for having done so. That is the beauty of the three goals. It can combat both of these attitudes if you set them up correctly.

The three goals you set MUST be things you know you can accomplish, even when you are motivated to do more, and/or not motivated to do anything at all. It sets the bar low at first, to get your mind and body used to the new structure. It's like bodybuilding. You don't start with 50 pound weights or 3 pound weights when you start out, you start with something you know you can pickup for a good three sets. As time goes on, you can increase the resistance slowly, so you don't injure yourself. After you accomplish your three goals, you are rewarded with the rest of the day to yourself without feeling any guilt. It truly is a life-saver so choose your goals wisely and just start.

4) Focus on one main project and make it good.

Of the myriad of advice I was given, one stood out to me the most. Focus on one big project, master it, and show it off to potential employers. It may not be for everyone, but this was the best piece of advice for me because it asked to do the one thing that I love, and that is to code. So, I just started coding. Not much of a solid app idea in my mind, but I chose something I knew I would enjoy working on in a programming language that I was excited to learn more of.

It also got the pressure off of me to do algos. It forced me to have one large goal that I knew over time I could accomplish one small step at a time. Of course it wasn't easy, I had my work cut out for me. The vision I had for my app was pretty hefty, but I was motivated and even when I wasn't, I looked forward to working on new code. My love for technical problem-solving helped me to create great new features for my website all of which I learned all on my own.

An added bonus was that it increased my confidence when talking with managers at interviews. I struggled with confidence from the beginning but with spending time off from interviewing and coming back with something to show for it, I was more confident. I worked on the one thing I thought I was lacking, and that was competence. I increased my competency in this one language and knew I could speak well about it. It also gave me the experience of learning new and difficult code that I didn't learn at camp. How I was able to problem-solve when debugging, which happened quite often, and overcame obstacles in my code. I was proud of my website and proud of what I was able to do.

5) Don't underestimate yourself.

I heard once that female coders struggle with this the most. That they often tend to underestimate their abilities with code and feel they aren't as knowledgeable as their male counterparts. I'm not sure if it is true, but I know that I underestimate my ability as a coder often and sometimes feel that I am not educated enough. Even though I spend countless hours learning new code and have created great applications, imposter syndrome is real. It also can be discouraging when the one key flaw you think you have, which is no experience, is the reason you didn't get the job.

It's an uphill battle, for sure, I know. We all struggle with it. Even though I feel like an imposter sometimes, I know deep down that I am qualified, even if my mind tries to convince me otherwise. The amount of learning we had to undertake in such a short amount of time is an outstanding feat and a testament to our ability to overcome. Only four (now 6) months ago we did not know what an object was and we definitely didn't know what algos were. I remember trying to explain what algos were before the camp. Boy, was I wrong.

In the end, we are all amazing for having gone through such an intense curriculum and having graduated is something we should all be proud of.

6) "Whatever time it takes, is time well spent".

If you're not feeling like you can even approach code, thats okay. If you feel like you can't take any advice from anyone, that is understandable too. One of the best mantras I have repeated over and over again as I was pulling out my hair from debugging my website or crying over my laptop was this,

whatever time it takes is time well spent.

It's the best mantra of all time, especially for coders. There were so many times I was at my code for over five hours straight trying to set one feature, only to find that I missed one variable. Or figuring out that the feature I wanted and worked on for three days was too advanced and I had to abandon it. I was at the point of tears often, but I kept repeating this mantra knowing that in the end it would all be worth it. Even though you can't see the next step in the horizon, you have to trust that it is there.

That time you think you wasted on code for three hours or three days, it was time well spent. That time you debugged only to find that the fix broke another piece of code, was time well spent. That code that you had to start from scratch all over again was time well spent. All of it. That is because you learned something. Even if you learned that that you didn't learn, you still learned something . Through all of it, you are gaining experience as a coder and that will always be worthwhile, whatever the outcome.

You cannot increase muscle without resistance and you cannot become a better coder without making mistakes and failing. Failing is the best way to become a great coder.

I hope this helps someone who as it did me. Learning these lessons is what life is all about. Failing into success, because that is how you succeed.
Items.Author: jenniferehala

Items.Title: 读书俱乐部：“测试驱动开发：示例”#9 | Book Club: "Test Driven Development: By Example" #9
Items.Description:
在 TDD 中，我们不问“我们要如何实现X ”，而是“我们要如何测试X ”。

我们在本章中使用了工厂，因此Dollar和Franc构造函数实际上可以相同 - 并允许将实现推送到超类。 拥有对两个基本相同但不同的类的重构经验以及测试的透明度，允许对每个重构中的步骤大小进行一些实验； 这里的设计和 TDD 过程是一个既能仔细工作又能更快工作的例子。

在此过程中，我的代码更改流程被旁边的某些东西打断了（我们一直在使用构造函数而不是.times()方法中的工厂）。 其他任何事情都应该放在待办事项列表中。

🔎 View my code-along repo at https://github.com/ruthmoog/test-driven-development-by-example 🔎 在https://github.com/ruthmoog/test-driven-development-by-example查看我的代码仓库

Kent Beck's " Test Driven Development: By Example " was released in 2002. The book aims to explain how to use TDD to write quality code that works. Kent Beck 的“测试驱动开发：通过示例”于 2002 年发布。该书旨在解释如何使用 TDD 编写有效的高质量代码。



原文:

In TDD we don't ask "how do we want to implement X", but "how do we want to test for X".

We utilised the factories in this chapter, so that the Dollar and Franc constructors could be effectively the same - and allow for the implementation to be pushed up to the super class. Having the experience of the refactor on two classes which are essentially the same-but-different along with the transparency from the tests, allowed some experimentation with the size of the steps in each refactor; the design and TDD process here is an example of both being able to work carefully, and work faster.

TDD is a steering process ... There is no right size step, now and forever.

Along the way, the flow of my code change was interrupted by something on the side (we'd been using the constructor instead of the factory in the .times() method). Beck introduces a rule credited to Jim Coplien, to allow one brief interruption but never an interruption to an interruption. Anything else should go on the To Do list.

🔎 View my code-along repo at https://github.com/ruthmoog/test-driven-development-by-example

Kent Beck's "Test Driven Development: By Example" was released in 2002. The book aims to explain how to use TDD to write quality code that works.
Items.Author: ruthmoog

Items.Title: 我创建自己的主页！ | I create my own homepage!
Items.Description:
重建投资组合。

  1. 高速
  2. 可扩展
  3. 最先进的技术

高速

我们使用Next.js作为我们的框架来做 SG。

成为 Vercel 的狗令人沮丧，但我会忍受的。 这是因为能够轻松优化字体和图像很有吸引力。

虽然叫SG，但是JS是在每一个页面过渡的时候运行的，所以运动是平滑的（也就是说，不是纯粹的）。

图像的大小与显示速度直接相关，因此非常有用。

可扩展

自始至终

组件设计基于原子设计，一种称为组件驱动开发的实践，因此易于重用和扩展。

网页

为了使信息尽可能保持最新，并且在更改信息时不直接提交到此存储库，我们构建了一个 API 服务器并通过它交换信息。

博客页面

这很有趣，因为它是使用各种统一 API 的组合构建的。

最先进的技术

我喜欢。 是的，我喜欢最先进的技术。 以下是我俱乐部 LT 演讲的节选，列出了该产品组合中使用的技术。

前端

我使用 Urql 作为我的 GraphQL 客户端。

托管由 Vercel（我是一只狗，汪汪汪）。

后端

我正在使用瞻博网络。 你可能已经注意到我正在尝试用Rust编写一个 GraphQL 服务器。

又难了。 我想马上逃到Nest.js。

我要把它渲染部署到（Heroku？那个家伙是......）。 我仍在试图弄清楚如何使用 GraphQL。

其他

这次我尝试使用umami进行分析。 用户界面真的很可爱，我喜欢它。

特征

在下文中，我们将描述我们能够实现的具体功能以及我们使用的库。

我不认为 Tailwind CSS 是正义的。 但是，我认为这是解决方案之一。

另外，由于我采用了原子设计，我认为在 JS 中使用 CSS 开发是最好的选择，所以我使用了 twin.macro。

它不支持 Tailwind CSS v3。

该问题仍然存在并正在受到监控。

自 Tailwind CSS v3 之后添加的flex-basis等特性自然不支持，因此采取了以下措施。

网址

您如何看待 GraphQL 客户端库？ 一些著名的是阿波罗和中继。 有一个名为@next/bundle-analyzer的库。 让我们构建它并查看捆绑包的大小。

你会对 Apollo 和 Relay 的规模感到惊讶。 你会惊讶于 Apollo 和 Relay 的大小，以及 Urql 相比起来有多么小。 如果你很好奇， Urql 会在这里做一个比较。

当然，它是正确位置的正确尺寸。 在这种情况下，我只需要一个查询，所以我把它做得尽可能小。

 /* pages/index.tsx */ // 略// eslint-disable-next-line unicorn/prevent-abbreviations export const getStaticProps : GetStaticProps < { meta : SeoProperties ; urqlState : SSRData } > = async () => { const client = await urqlClient (); await client . query ( HomeDocument ). toPromise (); const meta : SeoProperties = { description : " Rintaro Itokawa's Dev Site | re-taro " , ogImageUrl : encodeURI ( ` ${ OGP_HOST } /api/ogp?title=re-taro` ), pageRelPath : "" , pagetype : " website " , sitename : " re-taro.dev " , title : " Rintaro Itokawa - Emotion Seeker " , twcardtype : " summary_large_image " , }; return { props : { meta , urqlState : ssrCache . extractData (), }, }; }; const HomePage : NextPage < Properties > = ({ meta }) => { const [ response ] = useQuery < HomeQuery > ({ query : HomeDocument }); return < Home data = { response . data } meta = { meta } />; }; export default withUrqlClient ( () => ({ url : END_POINT , }), { neverSuspend : true , ssr : false }, )( HomePage );

它非常简单易用，尽管使用起来有点不寻常。

由remark-gfm支持。

 | Create | Table | | -------- | ---------- | | For | Example | | increase | Element. | - I can write - list. Also, [^1] [ ^1 ]: Footnotes can be used.
创造桌子
为了例子
增加元素。
  • 我可以写

表情符号

使用remakr-gemoji转换。

数值公式

咬一下remark-math和rehype-katex 。

只需加载样式表即可轻松设置。

 // components/organisms/post-meta/index.tsx // 略const PostMeta : React . FC < PostMetaPropeties > = ({ meta }) => ( < React . Fragment > < Seo { ... meta } /> < Head > < link rel = { " stylesheet " } href = { " https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.6/dist/katex.min.css " } integrity = { " sha384-ljao5I1l+8KYFXG7LNEA7DyaFvuvSCmedUf6Y6JI7LJqiu8q5dEivP2nDdFH31V4 " } crossOrigin = { " anonymous " } /> < title ></ title > </ Head > </ React . Fragment > ); // 略

红宝石

我使用了@haxibami 创建的remark- jaruby 。

另外， rehype-react相关流程如下。

 // lib/rehype-react.ts import React from " react " ; import rehypeParse from " rehype-parse " ; import rehypeReact from " rehype-react " ; import type { Options as RehypeReactOptions } from " rehype-react " ; import { unified } from " unified " ; import { Image } from " ~/components/molecules/image " ; import type { ImageProperties } from " ~/components/molecules/image " ; import { Link } from " ~/components/molecules/link " ; import type { LinkProperties } from " ~/components/molecules/link " ; // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any const RehypeReact = ( html : string ): React . ReactElement < unknown , string | React . JSXElementConstructor < any >> => { const result = unified () . use ( rehypeParse , { fragment : true , }) . use ( rehypeReact , { components : { // eslint-disable-next-line id-length a : ( properties : LinkProperties ) => Link ( properties ), img : ( properties : ImageProperties ) => Image ( properties ), }, createElement : React . createElement , } as RehypeReactOptions ) . processSync ( html ); return result . result ; }; export { RehypeReact };

以上使博客非常容易编写。

动态 OGP

我在查看@haxibami的实现时部署到 Vercel。

当存储数据的存储库更新时，动作会触发并向投资组合存储库发送一个调度事件，接收调度事件的存储库会自动构建数据。 我喜欢它，它很时尚。

调度员

name : Dispatch on : push jobs : build : runs-on : ubuntu-latest steps : - run : | curl -vv -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.DISPATCH_TOKEN }}" -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.everest-preview+json" "https://api.github.com/repos/re-taro/re-taro.dev/dispatches" -d '{"event_type": "update"}'

调度事件的接收者

name : Dispatch production build on : repository_dispatch : types : [ update ] jobs : // Jobs you want to run

展示次数

我很自豪地说，结果非常好。



原文:

Rebuilt portfolio.

  1. High Speed
  2. Scalable
  3. State-of-the-art technology

https://re-taro.dev <- This is the Portfolio I build!

https://github.com/re-taro/re-taro.dev <- And this is the repository!

High Speed

We are using Next.js as our framework to do SG.

It's frustrating to be Vercel's dog, but I'll put up with it. It is because it is attractive to be able to optimize font and images easily.

Although it is called SG, JS runs at each page transition, so the movement is smooth (in other words, it is not pure).

Cloudinary is used for image management to deliver optimized images.

The size of the images is directly related to the display speed, so it is very helpful.

Scalable

Throughout

The component design is based on Atomic Design, a practice known as Component Driven Development, so it is easy to reuse and extend.

Web Page

In order to keep the information as up-to-date as possible and not to leave direct commits to this repository when changing information, an API server was built and information was exchanged through it.

Blog Page

Markdown (content management) + tsx (template engine); Ramanujan said that Markdown would not go out of date so easily and that he felt comfortable throwing it into another service someday (I don't know, but...).

The Markdown processor used unified assets.

It was fun because it was built using a combination of various unified APIs.

State-of-the-art technology

I like it. Yes, I like state-of-the-art technology. Below is an excerpt from my club's LT talk, listing the technologies used in this portfolio.

Front-end

Next.js for framework, Tailwind CSS for styling (but I wanted to use CSS in JS, so I used twin.macro this time).

I'm using Urql as my GraphQL client.

Hosting is by Vercel (I'm a dog, woof woof).

Back-end

I'm using Juniper. You may have noticed that I am trying to write a GraphQL server in Rust.

It's difficult again. I want to escape to Nest.js right away.

I'm leaving it to render to deploy to (Heroku? That guy is...). I'm still trying to figure out what to do with GraphQL.

GraphQL is a justice, I thought while making it.

Others

I'm trying to use umami this time for analytics. The UI is really cute and I love it.

Feature

In the following, we will describe the specific functions we were able to implement and the libraries we used.

Tailwind CSS & twin.macro

I don't think Tailwind CSS is justice. However, I think it is one of the solutions.

Also, since I adopted Atomic Design, I thought that developing with CSS in JS was the best choice, so I used twin.macro.

There was one major problem with twin.macro. It does not support Tailwind CSS v3.

The issue is still standing and is being monitored.

The flex-basis and other features added since Tailwind CSS v3 are naturally not supported, so the following action was taken.

Urql

What do you think of GraphQL client libraries? Some well-known ones are Apollo and Relay. There is a library called @next/bundle-analyzer. Let's build it and look at the bundle size.

You will be surprised at the size of Apollo and Relay. You will be surprised at the size of Apollo and Relay, and how small Urql is in comparison. If you're curious, Urql does a comparison here.

Of course, it is the right size for the right place. In this case, I only needed a query, so I made it as small as possible.

/* pages/index.tsx */ // 略 // eslint-disable-next-line unicorn/prevent-abbreviations
export const getStaticProps: GetStaticProps<{ meta: SeoProperties; urqlState: SSRData }> = async () => { const client = await urqlClient(); await client.query(HomeDocument).toPromise(); const meta: SeoProperties = { description: "Rintaro Itokawa's Dev Site | re-taro", ogImageUrl: encodeURI(`${OGP_HOST}/api/ogp?title=re-taro`), pageRelPath: "", pagetype: "website", sitename: "re-taro.dev", title: "Rintaro Itokawa - Emotion Seeker", twcardtype: "summary_large_image", }; return { props: { meta, urqlState: ssrCache.extractData(), }, };
}; const HomePage: NextPage<Properties> = ({ meta }) => { const [response] = useQuery<HomeQuery>({ query: HomeDocument }); return <Home data={response.data} meta={meta} />;
}; export default withUrqlClient( () => ({ url: END_POINT, }), { neverSuspend: true, ssr: false },
)(HomePage);

It was very simple and easy to use, although a little unusual to use.

GitHub Flavored Markdown

Supported by remark-gfm.

| Create | Table |
| -------- | ---------- |
| For | Example |
| increase | Element. |

- I can write
 - list. Also, [^1] [^1]: Footnotes can be used.
Create Table
For Example
increase Element.
  • I can write

Emoji

Convert with remakr-gemoji.

:v: convert to ✌️.

Numerical Formula

Bite the remark-math and rehype-katex.

It is easy to set up just by loading the style sheet.

// components/organisms/post-meta/index.tsx // 略 const PostMeta: React.FC<PostMetaPropeties> = ({ meta }) => ( <React.Fragment> <Seo {...meta} /> <Head> <link rel={"stylesheet"} href={"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.6/dist/katex.min.css"} integrity={"sha384-ljao5I1l+8KYFXG7LNEA7DyaFvuvSCmedUf6Y6JI7LJqiu8q5dEivP2nDdFH31V4"} crossOrigin={"anonymous"} /> <title></title> </Head> </React.Fragment>
); // 略

ruby

I used remark-jaruby created by @haxibami.

The chain of methods around remark-parse / remark-rehype is as follows...

// utils/parser.ts import rehypeShiki from "@re-taro/rehype-shiki";
import rehypeAutolinkHeadings from "rehype-autolink-headings";
import rehypeKatex from "rehype-katex";
import rehypeSlug from "rehype-slug";
import rehypeStringify from "rehype-stringify";
import remarkGemoji from "remark-gemoji";
import remarkGfm from "remark-gfm";
import remarkJaruby from "remark-jaruby";
import remarkMath from "remark-math";
import remarkParse from "remark-parse";
import remarkRehype from "remark-rehype";
import remarkToc from "remark-toc";
import remarkUnwrapImages from "remark-unwrap-images";
import * as shiki from "shiki";
import stripMarkdown from "strip-markdown";
import { unified } from "unified"; const MdToHtml = async (md: string) => { const result = await unified() .use(remarkParse) .use(remarkGfm) .use(remarkGemoji) .use(remarkMath) .use(remarkJaruby) .use(remarkUnwrapImages) .use(remarkToc, { heading: "目次", tight: true, }) .use(remarkRehype) .use(rehypeKatex) .use(rehypeShiki, { highlighter: await shiki.getHighlighter({ theme: "nord" }), }) .use(rehypeSlug) .use(rehypeAutolinkHeadings, { behavior: "wrap", }) .use(rehypeStringify) .process(md); return result.toString();
}; export { MdToHtml }

In addition, the rehype-react related process is as follows.

// lib/rehype-react.ts import React from "react";
import rehypeParse from "rehype-parse";
import rehypeReact from "rehype-react";
import type { Options as RehypeReactOptions } from "rehype-react";
import { unified } from "unified";
import { Image } from "~/components/molecules/image";
import type { ImageProperties } from "~/components/molecules/image";
import { Link } from "~/components/molecules/link";
import type { LinkProperties } from "~/components/molecules/link"; // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
const RehypeReact = (html: string): React.ReactElement<unknown, string | React.JSXElementConstructor<any>> => { const result = unified() .use(rehypeParse, { fragment: true, }) .use(rehypeReact, { components: { // eslint-disable-next-line id-length a: (properties: LinkProperties) => Link(properties), img: (properties: ImageProperties) => Image(properties), }, createElement: React.createElement, } as RehypeReactOptions) .processSync(html); return result.result;
}; export { RehypeReact };

The above made the blog very easy to write.

dynamic OGP

I deployed to Vercel while looking at @haxibami's implementation.

https://github.com/re-taro/ogp.re-taro.dev <- This is the repository!

CI/CD

When the repository where the data is stored is updated, actions fires and sends a dispatch event to the portfolio repository, and the one that receives the dispatch event automatically builds the data. I like it, it's quite stylish.

Dispatcher

name: Dispatch
on: push jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - run: | curl -vv -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.DISPATCH_TOKEN }}" -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.everest-preview+json" "https://api.github.com/repos/re-taro/re-taro.dev/dispatches" -d '{"event_type": "update"}'

Receiver of the dispatch event

name: Dispatch production build on: repository_dispatch: types: [update] jobs: // Jobs you want to run

Impressions

I am proud to say that it turned out crazy good.
Items.Author: re_taro

Items.Title: 在 AWS 中完成 OpenVPN 设置 | Complete OpenVPN setup in AWS
Items.Description:
使用 SSH 和加密访问您的公共 EC2 实例很好。 但是如何使用基于 AWS 的非公开数据呢？ 管理员将此类资源置于公众无法接触到的地方的原因有很多。 但是，如果您无法在需要时找到它们，它们可能对您有什么好处？

顾名思义，OpenVPN 是一个开源项目，您始终可以下载免费的社区版并在您自己的 VPN 服务器上进行设置。 但是 OpenVPN 公司还提供了一个 OpenVPN 访问服务器作为 EC2 AMI。

费用是多少？
如果您只是进行测试并且不打算一次使用两个以上的连接访问 VPN，那么 AMI 本身是免费的。

首先，我们正在部署一个禁用自动分配公共 IP 的 Linux AMI 实例。

我们将无法通过 ssh 访问它，因为它没有分配公共 IP（如下图所示）。

启动 OpenVPN 访问服务器

可从 AWS Marketplace 获得 OpenVPN 访问服务器 AMI。

当涉及到实例类型时，我们可以将其保留在免费层内。

选择一个子网并记下以备后用

现在，安全组页面是 OpenVPN AMI 设置真正发挥作用的地方。 我们看到了一个安全组，它可以打开我们需要的一切。

注意：如果可行，收紧这些规则通常是个好主意，以便只接受来自有效公司 IP 地址范围的请求，但这对于短期测试来说很好。

然后，按 Enter 键进入所有默认要求。

请注意管理 UI 和客户端 UI。

访问服务器 将公共 IP 地址粘贴到终端中，作为调用为此实例设置的密钥对的 SSH 命令的一部分。

ssh -i KeyPairName.pem openvpnas@ ssh -i KeyPairName.pem openvpnas@



原文:

Accessing your public EC2 instances using SSH and encrypting is fine. But what about working with AWS-based data that’s not public? There are all kinds of reasons why admins keep such resources out of reach of the general public. But if you can’t get at them when you need, what good are they likely to do you?

The OpenVPN Access Server
As the name suggests, OpenVPN is an open source project, and you’re always able to download the free community edition and set things up on your own VPN server. But the OpenVPN company also provides an OpenVPN Access Server as an EC2 AMI .

What does it cost?
If you’re only testing things out and don’t plan to access the VPN using more than two connections at a time, then the AMI itself is free.

First of all we are deploying a Linux AMI instance with disabled Auto-assign Public IP.

We will be unable to ssh into it as it has no public IP assigned (as shown in the picture below).

Launching an OpenVPN Access Server

OpenVPN Access Server AMIs available from the AWS Marketplace.

When it comes to instance type, We can keep it within the free tier.

Choose a subnet and note for later

Now the Security Group page is where the OpenVPN AMI settings really shine. We’re presented with a security group that opens up everything we’ll need.

Note: If practical, it would normally be a good idea to tighten those rules so only requests from valid company IP address ranges are accepted, but this will be fine for short-term testing.

Then, press Enter to all the default requirements.

Note the Admin UI and Client UI.

Accessing the server Paste the public IP address into the terminal as part of SSH command that calls the key pair set for this instance.

ssh -i KeyPairName.pem openvpnas@
Items.Author: pranjalagrawal

Items.Title: Kubernetes 教程 - 静态 Pod 解释 | Kubernetes tutorial - Static Pods Explained
Items.Description:
你准备好享受 Kubernetes 的乐趣了吗？ 你知道几分钟的 Kubernetes 教程不会浪费。

好吧，让我们从一个 Kubernetes 流行语开始——Pods 。

您还可以将存储安装到您的 pod。 并且 Pod 有自己的临时 IP。 和更多。 但我们不要迷失在 Pod 定义的细节中。 我们的目标是了解什么是静态 Pod。

首先，让我们看一个使用kubectl创建 pod 的简单示例。

 root:/home/ubuntu/pods# cat test-pod.yaml apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: labels: run: test-pod name: test-pod spec: containers: - args: - sleep - "3600" image: busybox name: test-pod root:/home/ubuntu/pods# kubectl apply -f test-pod.yaml pod/test-pod created root:/home/ubuntu/pods# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE test-pod 1/1 Running 0 20s

这里没什么特别的 - 一个 pod 资源是使用一个简单的 yaml 文件创建的，该文件具有 pod 规范。

但不明显的是kubectl已经与 Kubernetes API Server（也称为Kube-apiserver ）交互，后者又与kube-scheduler组件通信以找到最佳节点来创建 pod 资源，然后指示kubelet代理坐在那个特定的节点上创建 pod。 然后所有任务详细信息都存储在etcd数据库中。 这是最常发生的典型工作流程。

顺便说一句，有几种方法可以验证我；）

订阅我的 YouTube 频道

在 LinkedIn 上与我联系

来源： Kubernetes 组件 | 虽然（普通）pod 是由 kubelet 响应 kube-apiserver 的指令创建的，但 kubelet 本身可以自行创建和管理 pod。 由 kubelet 管理的 pod 类型称为静态 pod 。

为了进一步了解，我们将首先查看 kubelet 服务的配置方式。 然后我们将看到如何获取 kubelet 代理来创建静态 pod。

我的测试/实验室 Kubernetes 集群在 Ubuntu 操作系统上运行。 我相信任何 linux 发行版都应该与我们将在这里看到的非常相似。 为了清楚起见，我们正在观察我的一个工作节点，通常在其中创建 Kubernetes 资源。

 root:/home/ubuntu# ps -ef|grep kubelet root 820 1 2 May21 ? 00:12:57 /usr/bin/kubelet --bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml --hostname-override=node1 --network-plugin=cni --pod-infra-container-image=k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2

更具体地说，键值对。 我指的是下面突出显示的staticPodPath 。

 root:/home/ubuntu# cat /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1 authentication: anonymous: enabled: false webhook: cacheTTL: 0s enabled: true x509: clientCAFile: /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt authorization: mode: Webhook webhook: cacheAuthorizedTTL: 0s cacheUnauthorizedTTL: 0s cgroupDriver: cgroupfs clusterDNS: - 10.96.0.10 clusterDomain: cluster.local cpuManagerReconcilePeriod: 0s evictionPressureTransitionPeriod: 0s fileCheckFrequency: 0s healthzBindAddress: 127.0.0.1 healthzPort: 10248 httpCheckFrequency: 0s imageMinimumGCAge: 0s kind: KubeletConfiguration logging: {} nodeStatusReportFrequency: 0s nodeStatusUpdateFrequency: 0s resolvConf: /run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf rotateCertificates: true runtimeRequestTimeout: 0s shutdownGracePeriod: 0s shutdownGracePeriodCriticalPods: 0s staticPodPath: /etc/kubernetes/manifests streamingConnectionIdleTimeout: 0s syncFrequency: 0s volumeStatsAggPeriod: 0s

创建静态 pod 的一种方法

我们可以简单地在目录 ' /etc/kubernetes/manifests ' 中创建一个包含静态 pod 定义的 pod yaml 文件。 让我们试试看。

 # list the pods prior to creating a pod yaml file root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE test-pod 1/1 Running 0 21m # this is the file I created in the directory we found before. root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# cat static-pod.yaml apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: labels: run: static-pod name: static-pod spec: containers: - args: - sleep - "3600" image: busybox name: static-pod # static pod gets created. kubelet appends the node name to the pod name. Interesting. root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE static-pod-node1 1/1 Running 0 26s test-pod 1/1 Running 0 21m

如果我们删除 manifests 目录中的 yaml 文件会怎样？

 root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# rm static-pod.yaml root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE static-pod-node1 1/1 Running 0 8m51s test-pod 1/1 Running 0 30m root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE test-pod 1/1 Running 0 30m

好吧，静态 pod 会自动删除。

另一种创建静态 pod 的方法

或者，我们可以在不同的目录中创建 pod yaml 文件，如下所示。

 root: /etc/kubelet.d # cat static-pod.yaml apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: labels: run: static-pod name: static-pod spec: containers: - args: - sleep - "3600" image: busybox name: static-pod

如果我们使用 systemd 命令观察 kubelet 服务的状态，我们将看到一个来自Drop-In 目录的附加配置文件正在使用。 我认为这个目录是一个包含特别是 kubelet 服务的覆盖配置的地方。

 root@ip-172-31-27-42:/etc/kubelet.d# systemctl status kubelet.service ● kubelet.service - kubelet: The Kubernetes Node Agent Loaded: loaded (/lib/systemd/system/kubelet.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled) Drop-In: /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d └─10-kubeadm.conf Active: active (running) since Sat 2022-05-21 19:02:44 UTC; 10h ago Docs: https://kubernetes.io/docs/home/

让我们挖掘这个文件 - /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf

 root:/etc/kubelet.d# cat /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf # Note: This dropin only works with kubeadm and kubelet v1.11+ [Service] Environment="KUBELET_KUBECONFIG_ARGS=--bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf" Environment="KUBELET_CONFIG_ARGS=--config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml" # This is a file that "kubeadm init" and "kubeadm join" generates at runtime, populating the KUBELET_KUBEADM_ARGS variable dynamically EnvironmentFile=-/var/lib/kubelet/kubeadm-flags.env # This is a file that the user can use for overrides of the kubelet args as a last resort. Preferably, the user should use # the .NodeRegistration.KubeletExtraArgs object in the configuration files instead. KUBELET_EXTRA_ARGS should be sourced from this file. EnvironmentFile=- /etc/default/kubelet ExecStart= ExecStart=/usr/bin/kubelet $KUBELET_KUBECONFIG_ARGS $KUBELET_CONFIG_ARGS $KUBELET_KUBEADM_ARGS $KUBELET_EXTRA_ARGS

让我们在“ /etc/default/kubelet ”文件中添加一个额外的参数，这样 kubelet 将使用新的清单路径启动。 并重启 kubelet 服务。 您将看到创建了“static-pod-node1”。

 #Do this on a worker node #Add new pod manifest path root:/etc/kubelet.d# cat /etc/default/kubelet KUBELET_EXTRA_ARGS="--pod-manifest-path=/etc/kubelet.d/" #list pods root:/etc/kubelet.d# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE test-pod 1/1 Running 0 47m #restart kubelet service root:/etc/kubelet.d# systemctl restart kubelet.service root:/etc/kubelet.d# kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE static-pod-node1 1/1 Running 0 14s test-pod 1/1 Running 0 52m #Observe kubelet was restarted with the new argument you added. root:/etc/kubelet.d# ps -ef|grep kubelet root 20820 1 13 06:13 ? 00:00:00 /usr/bin/kubelet --bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml --hostname-override=node1 --network-plugin=cni --pod-infra-container-image=k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2 --pod-manifest-path=/etc/kubelet.d/

顺便说一句，你知道我们之前看到的控制平面组件在名为 kube-system 的命名空间中作为 pod 运行吗？

 root:/home/ubuntu# kubectl get pods -n kube-system NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE etcd-master 1/1 Running 8 (5d11h ago) 53d kube-apiserver-master 1/1 Running 8 (5d11h ago) 53d kube-controller-manager-master 1/1 Running 8 (5d11h ago) 53d kube-scheduler-master 1/1 Running 8 (5d11h ago) 53d

至此，我们了解到正常的 Pod 是由 kubelet 根据 kube-apiserver 的指令创建的。 但是谁告诉 kubelet 创建 kube-apiserver pod？ 显然它不会是 kube-apiserver，因为它不存在。

好吧，kube-apiserver 和其他控制平面组件的 pod 确实是由 kubelet 创建为静态 pod。 因为在安装过程中，这些组件的 YAML 文件是在 /etc/kubernetes/manifests 目录下创建的。 现在您知道为什么我们需要静态 pod。 :) :)

 root:/home/ubuntu/pods# ls -l /etc/kubernetes/manifests/ total 16 -rw------- 1 root root 2209 Mar 29 14:49 etcd.yaml -rw------- 1 root root 3854 Mar 29 14:49 kube-apiserver.yaml -rw------- 1 root root 3251 Mar 29 14:49 kube-controller-manager.yaml -rw------- 1 root root 1435 Mar 29 14:49 kube-scheduler.yaml


原文:

Are you ready for some Kubernetes fun? You know a few minutes on a Kubernetes tutorial wouldn't be a waste.

Alright, let's start with a Kubernetes buzzword - Pods.

Pods are the most granular resource you can manage in a Kubernetes cluster by using Kubernetes control plane. Pods can contain one or more containers, where one will deploy applications. You may also mount storage to your pods. And the pods have their own ephemeral IPs. And more. But let's not get lost in the details of a Pod definition. Our goal is to understand what Static Pods are.

First, let's take a look at a simple example of creating a pod using kubectl.

root:/home/ubuntu/pods# cat test-pod.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    run: test-pod
  name: test-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - sleep
    - "3600"
    image: busybox
    name: test-pod

root:/home/ubuntu/pods# kubectl apply -f test-pod.yaml
pod/test-pod created

root:/home/ubuntu/pods# kubectl get pods
NAME       READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
test-pod   1/1     Running   0          20s

Nothing fancy going on here - a pod resource was created using a simple yaml file which has the pod specifications.

But what is not obvious is that kubectl has interacted with Kubernetes API Server (also referred to as Kube-apiserver) which in turn communicates with a kube-scheduler component to find the best node to create the pod resource on and then instructs a kubelet agent sitting on that particular node to create the pod. Then all the task details are stored in an etcd database. This is a typical workflow that happens mostly.

By the way, a couple of ways to validate me ;)

Subscribe to my YouTube channel

Connect with me on LinkedIn

The components like kube-apiserver, kube-scheduler, kube-controller-manager, and etcd database together form the control plane of a Kubernetes cluster.

While the (normal) pods are created in response to the instruction from kube-apiserver by kubelet, kubelet itself can create and manage pods on its own. The type of pods that are managed by a kubelet is called static pods.

Kubelet Service

To understand further, we will start by looking at how a kubelet service is configured. Then we will see how to get a kubelet agent to create a static pod.

My test/lab Kubernetes cluster runs on Ubuntu operating system. I believe any linux distribution should look pretty similar to what we are going to see here. Just to be clear, we are observing one of my worker nodes, where Kubernetes resources are commonly created. 

root:/home/ubuntu# ps -ef|grep kubelet
root       820     1  2 May21 ?        00:12:57 /usr/bin/kubelet --bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml --hostname-override=node1 --network-plugin=cni --pod-infra-container-image=k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2

/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml file is particular important because it contains a line that will help us to create static pods. More specifically, a key-value pair. I am referring to staticPodPath highlighted below.

root:/home/ubuntu# cat /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml
apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
authentication: anonymous: enabled: false webhook: cacheTTL: 0s enabled: true x509: clientCAFile: /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt
authorization: mode: Webhook webhook: cacheAuthorizedTTL: 0s cacheUnauthorizedTTL: 0s
cgroupDriver: cgroupfs
clusterDNS:
- 10.96.0.10
clusterDomain: cluster.local
cpuManagerReconcilePeriod: 0s
evictionPressureTransitionPeriod: 0s
fileCheckFrequency: 0s
healthzBindAddress: 127.0.0.1
healthzPort: 10248
httpCheckFrequency: 0s
imageMinimumGCAge: 0s
kind: KubeletConfiguration
logging: {}
nodeStatusReportFrequency: 0s
nodeStatusUpdateFrequency: 0s
resolvConf: /run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf
rotateCertificates: true
runtimeRequestTimeout: 0s
shutdownGracePeriod: 0s
shutdownGracePeriodCriticalPods: 0s
staticPodPath: /etc/kubernetes/manifests
streamingConnectionIdleTimeout: 0s
syncFrequency: 0s
volumeStatsAggPeriod: 0s

One way to create a static pod

We can simply create a pod yaml file, which has the static pod definition, in the directory '/etc/kubernetes/manifests'. Kubelet will create the static pod shortly. Let's try that.

# list the pods prior to creating a pod yaml file
root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods
NAME               READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
test-pod           1/1     Running   0          21m

# this is the file I created in the directory we found before.
root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# cat static-pod.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    run: static-pod
  name: static-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - sleep
    - "3600"
    image: busybox
    name: static-pod

# static pod gets created. kubelet appends the node name to the pod name. Interesting.
root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods
NAME               READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
static-pod-node1   1/1     Running   0          26s
test-pod           1/1     Running   0          21m

What if we removed the yaml file in the manifests directory?

root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# rm static-pod.yaml

root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods
NAME               READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
static-pod-node1   1/1     Running   0          8m51s
test-pod           1/1     Running   0          30m

root:/etc/kubernetes/manifests# kubectl get pods
NAME       READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
test-pod   1/1     Running   0          30m

Well, the static pod gets removed automatically.

Another way to create a static pod

Alternatively, we can create the pod yaml file in a different directory as shown below.

root:/etc/kubelet.d# cat static-pod.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    run: static-pod
  name: static-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - sleep
    - "3600"
    image: busybox
    name: static-pod

If we observe the status of kubelet service using a systemd command, we will see an additional configuration file being used from a Drop-In directory. I think of this directory as a place that contains the override configuration of particularly kubelet service.

root@ip-172-31-27-42:/etc/kubelet.d# systemctl status kubelet.service
● kubelet.service - kubelet: The Kubernetes Node Agent Loaded: loaded (/lib/systemd/system/kubelet.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
 Drop-In: /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d └─10-kubeadm.conf
   Active: active (running) since Sat 2022-05-21 19:02:44 UTC; 10h ago
     Docs: https://kubernetes.io/docs/home/

Let's dig this file - /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf

root:/etc/kubelet.d# cat /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf
# Note: This dropin only works with kubeadm and kubelet v1.11+
[Service]
Environment="KUBELET_KUBECONFIG_ARGS=--bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf"
Environment="KUBELET_CONFIG_ARGS=--config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml"
# This is a file that "kubeadm init" and "kubeadm join" generates at runtime, populating the KUBELET_KUBEADM_ARGS variable dynamically
EnvironmentFile=-/var/lib/kubelet/kubeadm-flags.env
# This is a file that the user can use for overrides of the kubelet args as a last resort. Preferably, the user should use
# the .NodeRegistration.KubeletExtraArgs object in the configuration files instead. KUBELET_EXTRA_ARGS should be sourced from this file.
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/default/kubelet
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/bin/kubelet $KUBELET_KUBECONFIG_ARGS $KUBELET_CONFIG_ARGS $KUBELET_KUBEADM_ARGS $KUBELET_EXTRA_ARGS

Let's add an extra argument to "/etc/default/kubelet" file such that kubelet will be started with a new manifest path. And restart kubelet service. You will see 'static-pod-node1' gets created.

#Do this on a worker node
#Add new pod manifest path
root:/etc/kubelet.d# cat /etc/default/kubelet
KUBELET_EXTRA_ARGS="--pod-manifest-path=/etc/kubelet.d/" #list pods
root:/etc/kubelet.d# kubectl get pods
NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE
test-pod 1/1 Running 0 47m #restart kubelet service
root:/etc/kubelet.d# systemctl restart kubelet.service root:/etc/kubelet.d# kubectl get pods
NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE
static-pod-node1 1/1 Running 0 14s
test-pod 1/1 Running 0 52m #Observe kubelet was restarted with the new argument you added.
root:/etc/kubelet.d# ps -ef|grep kubelet
root 20820 1 13 06:13 ? 00:00:00 /usr/bin/kubelet --bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml --hostname-override=node1 --network-plugin=cni --pod-infra-container-image=k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2 --pod-manifest-path=/etc/kubelet.d/

By the way, do you know the control plane components we looked at before run as pods as well in a namespace called kube-system.

root:/home/ubuntu# kubectl get pods -n kube-system
NAME                                      READY   STATUS    RESTARTS         AGE
etcd-master                               1/1     Running   8 (5d11h ago)    53d
kube-apiserver-master                     1/1     Running   8 (5d11h ago)    53d
kube-controller-manager-master            1/1     Running   8 (5d11h ago)    53d
kube-scheduler-master                     1/1     Running   8 (5d11h ago)    53d

At this point we understand the normal pods are created by kubelet as per the instruction from kube-apiserver. But then who told kubelet to create kube-apiserver pod? Obviously it would not have been kube-apiserver, because it didn't exist.

Well, kube-apiserver and other control plane components' pods are indeed created as static pods by kubelet. Because during the installation, the YAML files of these components are created in /etc/kubernetes/manifests directory. Now you know why we need static pods. :)

root:/home/ubuntu/pods# ls -l /etc/kubernetes/manifests/
total 16
-rw------- 1 root root 2209 Mar 29 14:49 etcd.yaml
-rw------- 1 root root 3854 Mar 29 14:49 kube-apiserver.yaml
-rw------- 1 root root 3251 Mar 29 14:49 kube-controller-manager.yaml
-rw------- 1 root root 1435 Mar 29 14:49 kube-scheduler.yaml

Items.Title: 如何选择优质的泰伯磨耗仪？ | How to choose a high-quality Taber abrasion tester？
Items.Description:
泰伯耐磨试验机主要是利用不同型号的摩擦轮在试样上旋转，通过旋转研磨的方式来测试皮革和塑料材料的耐磨性。 选择标准适用的重量组来测试材料特性。 检测样品外表面的重量损失，如明显的机械损伤。

 ![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/idlrmej0ye2chgxszow2.jpg)

参数： 试件：外径108mm，内径8mm，3mm（D） 砂轮：直径为2"(M*45mm) 1/2"(W) 砂轮中心距：63.5mm 磨削中心距轮子和测试盘：37～38mm 转速：60/72rpm 重物：250g、500g、1000g 计数器：LCD，0～999.999 体积：530×320×310+吸尘器 重量：18kg（不含吸尘器） 电源: 1φ, 220v/50HZ

磨损指数计算公式：磨损量/公式转数×100



原文:

The Taber abrasion resistance testing machine mainly uses different types of friction wheels to rotate on the sample to test the abrasion resistance of leather and plastic materials by means of rotary grinding. Select standard applicable weight sets to test material properties. Detect weight loss on the outer surface of the sample, such as obvious mechanical damage.


![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/idlrmej0ye2chgxszow2.jpg)

parameter: Test piece: outer diameter 108mm, inner diameter 8mm, 3mm (D) Grinding wheel: diameter is 2"(M*45mm) 1/2"(W) Wheel center spacing: 63.5mm The distance between the center of the grinding wheel and the test disc: 37～38mm Rotation speed: 60/72rpm Severe weight: 250g, 500g, 1000g Counter: LCD, 0～999.999 Volume: 530 × 320 × 310 + vacuum cleaner Weight: 18kg (without vacuum cleaner) Power supply: 1φ, 220v/50HZ

Calculation formula of wear index: wear loss weight / formula number of revolutions × 100
Items.Author: qinsunmarket

Items.Title: 一个使用 ReactJS + FireStore 的简单 meme 生成器 | A simple meme generator using ReactJS + FireStore
Items.Description:
这是我第一次参加 ReactJS 比赛/黑客马拉松，我没有获胜，但我真诚地花了大约 1 周的时间为它编写代码，我对结果非常满意。

挑战：要创建一个可以通过以下方式生成 meme 的 ReactJS 应用程序，左侧面板带有左侧标题和图像，右侧面板带有右侧标题和图像。 标题可以在图像的顶部或底部。

演示： https ://memegen-4c017.web.app/（只读，创建一个 firebase 项目以查看“保存 Meme”功能）

虽然 memegenerator 是一个非常小的项目，但以下代码可能对初学者有用：

  1. 将 TypeScript 与 ReactJS 一起使用
  2. 使用 ContextApi 在组件之间共享数据
  3. 使用 FireStore 存储和检索 meme 数据

我还想让网络应用程序响应，但还有很长的路要走。

该网站托管在 Firebase 托管上，现在 firebase 还提供 CI/CD github 集成选项。

开发代码使用自举

npx create-react-app memegen --template typescript

应用程序布局有标题和主要部分两部分。 主要部分可以在 CREATE 和 BROWSE 之间切换。

所有需要 props 的组件都遵循以下 TypeScript 约定：

 interface Props { appmode: MenuOptions, menuSelect: (option: MenuOptions) => void } const Header: React.FC<Props> = ({ appmode, menuSelect }) => { }

希望该项目可以帮助尝试学习 ReactJS 和 TypeScript 的人。

寻找一些反馈。 谢谢。



原文:

This was my first ever ReactJS competition/hackathon, I did not win but I sincerely spent around 1 week coding for it and I was pretty happy with the outcome.

The challenge : To create a ReactJS app that can generate a meme in the following way, a left panel with left heading and image and a right panel with the right heading and image. The heading can be on top of the image or the bottom.

Github repo : https://github.com/avithe1/memegenerator

Demo : https://memegen-4c017.web.app/ (Read-only, create a firebase project to see 'Save Meme' functionality)

Although memegenerator is a very small project, the following code could be useful for beginners:

  1. Use of TypeScript with ReactJS
  2. Use of ContextApi for data sharing among components
  3. Use of FireStore to store and retrieve meme data

I also wanted to make the web app responsive but it's a long way to go.

The website is hosted on Firebase hosting and now firebase also provides option for CI/CD github integration.

Development The code is bootstrapped using

npx create-react-app memegen --template typescript

The application layout has two parts the header and the main section. The main section can toggle between CREATE and BROWSE.

All components that require props follow the following TypeScript convention:

interface Props {
    appmode: MenuOptions,
    menuSelect: (option: MenuOptions) => void
}

const Header: React.FC<Props> = ({ appmode, menuSelect }) => {
}

Hope the project helps someone trying to learn ReactJS and TypeScript.

P.S. I am not a pro with TypeScript in fact this was my first attempt with TypeScript. Looking for some feedback. Thanks.

Items.Description:
阿吉克·塞蒂亚万

发表于使用react-facebook-pixel非常容易地集成 facebook 像素

打开并修改 _app.tsx 并添加如下代码

 useEffect(() => { import('react-facebook-pixel') .then((x) => x.default) .then((ReactPixel) => { ReactPixel.init('FACEBOOK_PIXEL_ID') ReactPixel.pageView() router.events.on('routeChangeComplete', () => { ReactPixel.pageView() }) }) }, [router.events])

詹姆斯'但丁'米兹 - Apr 21 4月21日



原文:

Agik Setiawan

Posted on May 22

Itegrate facebook pixel very easy with react-facebook-pixel

Open and modify _app.tsx and add code like below

 useEffect(() => {
    import('react-facebook-pixel')
      .then((x) => x.default)
      .then((ReactPixel) => {
        ReactPixel.init('FACEBOOK_PIXEL_ID')
        ReactPixel.pageView()

        router.events.on('routeChangeComplete', () => {
          ReactPixel.pageView()
        })
      })
  }, [router.events])

Items.Title: Laravel 9 成为最受欢迎的 PHP 框架的 10 大理由-Codexashish | Top 10 Reasons Why Laravel 9 Is The Most Popular PHP Framework-Codexashish
Items.Description:
我们将在这里定义 Laravel 框架的一些引人注目的选项，这可以证明为什么它获得了最高质量。

什么是框架？

框架可能是一个可重用的包，它被开发用来帮助开发人员生产他们的应用程序。 它提供了特定的实用性，人们在他们的程序中没有使用它来扩展功能和提高性能。 因此，像 Laravel 这样的包框架的目的是通过提供现成的解决方案来防止开发人员重新发明轮子。

例如：路由、身份验证、会话和缓存

什么是 Laravel 框架？

于 2011 年作为用于互联网应用程序开发的关联 ASCII 文本文件框架发布。 遵循模型-视图-控制器 (MVC) 美术模式。 拥有 66k+ 星的 GitHub 用户中最广泛认可的 PHP 框架。

提供改进的安全功能。

使用 Laravel 框架的第一个也是最大的优点是它遵循模型、视图、关联的基于控制器的美术模式，并且它是一种可交流的可爱语法，使其面向对象。 开发人员共同获得 20 多个预安装的标准和面向对象的库和辅助工具，用于开发强大的互联网应用程序

在 laravel 中，所有 Laravel 路由都在 app/Http/routes.php 文件中明确定义，该文件经常由框架加载。 最重要的基本 Laravel 路由基本上满足于 URI 和闭包，因此提供了一种简单的、可交流的路由声明技术。

也就是说，正是一些训练有素的工作人员指挥您的应用程序配备了安全的身份验证和授权。

在这些存储选择之间进行更改非常容易，因为每个系统的 API 都是相同的。 一个人将在一个应用程序中使用所有 3 个系统来提供来自多个位置的文件，例如在一个非常分散的环境中。

训练有素的员工的常见用途包括企业包资产、管理信息迁移、播种和为品牌生成新控制器、模型和迁移的样板代码。 此功能使开发人员无需制作正确的代码骨架。 一种是通过实施新的自定义命令来扩展训练有素的工作人员的实用性和能力。
